Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

SpTU 1, 001 [SB Nergal and Ereškigal][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
Column i
(1 column missing)
Column ii
(start of column missing)
o ii 1'o 1'

[e-rid ana] ⸢gišTIR⸣ gišMES i-kis gišMES giš⸢ti-ʾa-a-lu⸣ [u gišsu-pa-a-lu]

(o ii 1') '[Go down to] the mēsu-forest, cut down a mēsu-tree, a white cedar, [and a juniper, (and) break off] kanaktu and sibbirru branches.'

o ii 2'2'

[ḫu-uṣ-ṣib] ⸢giš⸣ka-na-ka-a-tu₄ u gišsim-bir-ri

o ii 3'3'

[...] x an-ni-tu₄ ina še-me-e-šu₂ : iš-ši ḫa-aṣ-ṣi-in-nu ina i-di-šu₂

(o ii 3') When he (Nergal) [...] heard this, he lifted the axe in his arms, (and) drew the sword from his belt.

o ii 4'4'

[iš]-lu-pu nam-ṣa-ri ina šip-pi-šu₂

o ii 5'5'

[u₂]-⸢rid⸣-ma ana gišTIR gišMES ik-kis :! (U) gišMES gišti-ʾa-a-lu u gišsu-pa-a-lu

(o ii 5') He went down to the mēsu forest, cut down a mēsu-tree, a white cedar, and a juniper, (and) broke off kanaktu and sibbirru branches.

o ii 6'6'

[u₂-ḫa-aṣ]-ṣib giška-na-ka-a-tu₄ u gišsim-bir-ri

o ii 7'7'

[...] x i-ban-na-a gišGU.ZA de₂-a dNIN.ŠI.KU₃

(o ii 7') [...] he created a throne of Ea and Niššiku. He painted it with gypsum [instead of] silver; he painted it with zagindurû-lapis lazuli instead of lapis lazuli; he made it multicoloured with orpiment (and) kalgukku-paste [instead of] gold; [...] completed - all of the throne.

o ii 8'8'

[me₂-eḫ-rat] KU₃.BABBAR IM.BABBAR u₂-taq-qa : me₂-eḫ-rat na₄ZA.GIN₃ na₄ZA.GIN₃.DURU₅ u₂-taq-qa

o ii 9'9'

[me₂-eḫ-rat] KU₃.SI₂₂ IM.GA₂.LI IM.KAL.GUG ub-<<MIN>>-tar-rim

o ii 10'10'

[...] qut-ta-a-tu₄ gišGU.ZA ka-la-mu

o ii 11'11'

[... ša₂-a]-šu₂ il-si-šu₂-ma i-šak-kan-šu₂ ṭe₃-e-mu

(o ii 11') [...] he (Ea) called him (Nergal) and gave him the (following) instructions:

o ii 12'12'

[...] ⸢ḫar⸣-ra-nu te-riš tal-lak : ul-tu ul-la-nu ina ka-ša₂-di-ka

(o ii 12') '[...] of the road, you wish to go. As soon as you arrive there [...] (when) a chair is offered to you, do not proceed to sit down on it; when [the cook] offers you bread, do not proceed to eat his bread; when [the butcher] offers you meat, do not proceed to eat his meat.'

o ii 13'13'

[... giš]⸢GU⸣.ZA na-šu-nik-ka e ta-ʾi-ir-ma e tu-šib ina UGU

o ii 14'14'

[lu₂MUḪALDIM] NINDA-ḪI.A na-ši-ka e ta-ʾi-ir-ma a-kal-šu₂ e ta-kul

o ii 15'15'

[lu₂GIR₂.LA₂] UZU na-ši-ka e ta-ʾi-ir-ma UZU-šu₂ e ta-kul

Column iii
(start of column missing)
o iii 1'o 1'

kam₂-su AŠ₂ x [...]

(o iii 1') kneeling ... [...]

o iii 2'2'

DINGIR-MEŠ ina IGI-ia ⸢A₂⸣ x [...]

(o iii 2') the gods ... [...] in my presence.

o iii 3'3'

e-nen-na it-tar-du ana KUR.NU.⸢GI₄⸣.[A ...]

(o iii 3') Now he has gone down to the Land Of No Return [...].

o iii 4'4'

dEREŠ.KI.GAL KA-šu₂ DU₃-ma DU₁₁.GA ana dNAM.TAR a-mat ⸢MU⸣[ar₂]

(o iii 4') Ereškigal opened her (lit. his) mouth and spoke. She said a word to Namtar:

o iii 5'5'

dNAM.TARri dEN.LIL₂u₂-tu₂ a tu-ba-ʾi

(o iii 5') 'Namtar, do not look for divine supremacy, and do not let your heart create heroism. Go up and sit down on the throne, the dais of kingship! You make the judgements of the wide Underworld, that I may go up to the heaven of Anu, my father, eat the bread of Anu, my father, (and) drink the beer of Anu, my father. Go, Namtar, and bring that god to me!'

o iii 6'6'

u qar-ra-du-ta a i-ban!(GIM-)na-aʾ lib₃-bu-uk-ku

o iii 7'7'

e-li-ma ti-šab ina gišGU.ZA BARA₂ LUGALu₂-tu₂

o iii 8'8'

di-ni er-ṣe-tu₄ ra-pa-aš₂-tu₄ i-pu-uš at-ta

o iii 9'9'

ana-ku ana ANe ša₂ d60 AD-ia lu-li-ma NINDA-ḪI.A d60 AD-ia lu-kul

o iii 10'10'

ši-kar d60 AD-ia lu-uš-tu₂

o iii 11'11'

a-lik-ma dNAM.TARri DINGIR-MEŠ ša₂-a-šu₂ šu-ri-bi ana maḫ-ri-ia

o iii 12'12'

dNAM.TARri il-lik-ma u₂-še-rib DINGIR-MEŠ dER₃.RA

(o iii 12') Namtar went and brought in the god Erra.

o iii 13'13'

1en KA₂ ša₂ dBI₂.DU₈ ina e-re-bi-šu₂

(o iii 13') When he entered the first gate of Bidu, ditto (when he entered) the second gate of Enkišar, ditto (when he entered) the third gate of Endašurimma, ditto (when he entered) the fourth gate of Nerulla, ditto (when he entered) the fifth gate of Nerubanda, ditto (when he entered) the sixth gate of Endukuga, ditto (when he entered) the seventh gate of Ennugigi, he entered her (lit. his) wide court.

o iii 14'14'

2 KA₂ ša₂ dEN.KI.ŠAR₂ ina MIN<(e-re-bi-šu₂)> : šal-šu₂ KA₂ ša₂ dEN.DA.ŠURIM.MA ina MIN<(e-re-bi-šu₂)>

Reverse
Column i
r i 1r i 1

4a KA₂ ša₂ dNE.RU.UL.LA ina e-re-bi-šu₂

r i 22

5šu₂ KA₂ ša₂ dNE.RU.BAN₃.DA ina MIN<(e-re-bi-šu₂)> : 6šu₂ KA₂ <ša₂> dEN.DU₆.KU₃.GA ina MIN<(e-re-bi-šu₂)>

r i 33

7a KA₂ ša₂ dEN.NU.GI₄.GI₄ ina MIN<(e-re-bi-šu₂)> : i-ru-um-ma ana pal-ki-i KISAL-šu₂

r i 44

ik-mis iš-šiq qaq-qar ma-ḫar-šu₂ : i-šir₃ iz-za-az-zu i-zak-kar-šu₂

(r i 4) He knelt (and) kissed the groud before her (lit. him). He straightened, stood (and) spoke to her (lit. him):

r i 55

um-ma d60 AD-ka iš-pur-a-ni ana ma-aḫ-ri-ka

(r i 5) 'Anu, your father, sent me to you saying: "Sit on this throne and make the judgements of the great gods, the great gods who dwell inside the Underworld." '

r i 66

um-ma ina gišGU.ZA an-ni-i ti-šab-ma di-ni di-i-ni ša₂ DINGIR-MEŠ GAL-MEŠ

r i 77

DINGIR-MEŠ GAL-MEŠ a-ši-bi qe₂-reb er-kal-lu₄

r i 88

ul-tu ul-la-nu ina ka-ša₂-di-šu₂

(r i 8) At the very moment that he arrived, a chair was offered to him, but he did not proceed to sit down on it; the cook offered him bread, but he did not proceed to eat his bread; the butcher offered him meat, but he did not proceed to eat his meat; the cup-bearer offered him beer, but he did not proceed [to drink his] beer; the cleaner offered him water (to wash his) feet, but [...].

r i 99

gišGU.ZA na-šu-niš-šu₂ ul ir-a-ma ul u₂-šib ina muḫ-ḫi

r i 1010

lu₂MUḪALDIM NINDA-ḪI.A na-ši-šu₂ ul ir-a-ma NINDA-ḪI.A-šu₂ ul i-kul

r i 1111

lu₂GIR₂.LA₂ UZU na-ši-šu₂ ul ir-a-ma UZU-šu₂ ⸢ul i-kul⸣

r i 1212

lu₂ŠU.DU₈.<SILA₃>.A KAŠ-ḪI.A na-ši-šu₂ ul ir-⸢a-ma KAŠ-ḪI.A⸣ [ul iš-ti]

r i 1313

lu₂me-se-e GIR₃-MIN me-e na-ši-šu₂ ⸢ul⸣ [...]

r i 1414

u ši-i!(ia) i-ru-⸢um-ma⸣ [...]

(r i 14) And then she entered [...], she purified herself [... her] body [...].

r i 1515

te-⸢lil₂⸣ x [...]

r i 1616

⸢zu-mur⸣-[šu₂? ...]

(rest of column missing)
Column ii
r ii 11

DUMU šip-ri ša₂ d60 AD-ia ša₂ il-lik-an-na-a-šu₂ NINDA-ḪI.A-ni li-kul ši-kar;ni  ; : : liš-ti

(r ii 1) 'Let the messenger of Anu, my father, who came to us, eat our bread (and) drink our beer.'

r ii 22

dNAM.TARri KA-šu₂ DU₃-ma DU₁₁.GA ana dEREŠ.KI.GAL a-mat MUar₂

(r ii 2) Namtar opened her (lit. his) mouth and spoke. She said a word to Ereškigal:

r ii 33

[...] x ša₂ d60 AD-ka ša₂ il-lik-an-na-a-šu₂ la-am ur-ra i-nam-mi-ru ša₂-da-šu₂  ; : : : i-te-lu

(r ii 3) '[...] ... of Anu, your father, who came to us, went up his mountain before the day became bright.'

r ii 44

[... d]⸢EREŠ⸣.KI.GAL uk-tab-bit-ma il-ta-si ma-lit

(r ii 4) Ereškigal's [...] became heavy and she uttered a loud cry. She fell from the throne to the ground, she got up from the ground, tears raining in her (lit. his) eyes. Her tears flowed [...] her (lit. his) [...]. 'Oh Erra, my delightful consort! [I did not yet become sated] with his sexual charms, but he went away! [Oh Erra], my delightful consort! I did not yet become sated with his sexual charms, but he went away!'

r ii 55

[ul]-⸢tu⸣ UGU gišGU.ZA ana qaq-qar in-taq-ut

r ii 66

[ul]-⸢tu⸣ qaq-qar u₂-še-šer₃ : ina IGI-MIN-šu₂ di-ma-tu₄ i-za-an-nun

r ii 77

[...] x-šu₂ il-la-ku di-ma-šu₂ : dER₃.RA ḫa-aʾ-i-ri  ; : : la-lu-u₂-a

r ii 88

[ul a-ša₂-ba-aʾ] ⸢la⸣-la-a-šu₂ it-tal-ka-a-ni

r ii 99

[dER₃.RA ḫa-aʾ-i]-⸢ri⸣ la-lu-u₂-a : ul a-ša₂-ba-aʾ la-la-a-šu₂ it-tal-ka;::- a-ni

r ii 1010

[dNAM.TARri KA-šu₂] DU₃-ma DU₁₁.GA ana dEREŠ.KI.GAL a-mat MUar₂

(r ii 10) [Namtar] opened [his mouth] and spoke. He said a word to Ereškigal:

r ii 1111

[...]-ka šu-pur-in-ni-ma DINGIR-MEŠ ša₂-a-šu₂ lu-uṣ-bat-tak-ma  ; lul-qa-ka ka-a-šu₂

(r ii 11) 'Send me [to ...], your [...], and let me seize that god and let me take him to you.'

r ii 1212

[dEREŠ.KI.GAL KA-šu₂ DU₃]-⸢ma⸣ DU₁₁.GA ana dNAM.TAR a-mat MUar₂

(r ii 12) [Ereškigal opened her mouth] and spoke. She said a word to Namtar:

r ii 1313

[...] ⸢ana⸣ d60 dEN.LIL₂ u dIDIM ki-a-am ta-qab!(TAG-)bi

(r ii 13) '[...] to Anu, Ellil and Ea, and say (to them) thus; [...] Ellil and Ea, and repeat (to them) thus: "[...] I [...] girl [...] of young women [...]." '

r ii 1414

[...] dEN.LIL₂ u dIDIM ki-a-am tu-ša₂-an-nu

r ii 1515

[...]-⸢ku⸣ ma-rat ana-ku

r ii 1616

[...] ša₂ KI.SIKIL-MEŠ

(rest of column missing)
Column iii
(1 column missing)
